name: llama-copilot
description: VS Code extension that integrates llama-server LLMs as language model chat providers.

llamaCopilot.endpoints.description: Configuration for llama-server endpoints. Each key is an endpoint identifier, and models will be suffixed with @<identifier> (e.g., model-name@local)

url.description: URL of the llama-server instance (e.g., http://localhost:8013)

apiToken.description: Optional API token for authenticated endpoints

headers.description: Optional headers to add to all requests for this endpoint

requestBody.description: Optional JSON properties to merge into request body for this endpoint

models.description: Per-model configuration. Each key is a model ID (without @endpoint suffix)

headers.description: Optional headers specific to this model (merged with endpoint headers, model headers take precedence)

requestBody.description: Optional JSON properties to merge into request body for this model (merged with endpoint requestBody, model properties take precedence)

contextSize.description: Override context size for this model (in tokens)

maxOutputTokens.description: Override maximum output tokens for this model

imageInput.description: Whether image input is supported by the model

toolCalling.description: Whether tool calling is supported. Can be a boolean or a number (maximum number of tools)

llamaCopilot.requestTimeoutSeconds.description: Request timeout in seconds for llama-server (headers and body). Increase for large contexts.

llamaCopilot.promptProgressStatusBarThre.description: Show status bar during prompt processing when estimated remaining time exceeds this many seconds. Set to 0 to disable. Prompt processing time is estimated assuming quadratic scaling with prompt length.

llamaCopilot.minPromptProgressElapsedMs.description: Minimum milliseconds of prompt processing before showing progress. Lower this to see progress on faster models. Set to 0 to show progress immediately.

llamaCopilot.enableCursorRules.description: Enable cursor rules tool for LLM access to project rules from .cursor/rules/

llamaCopilot.inlineCompletionModel.description: Model ID for inline (ghost) completions in the editor (e.g. sweep-next-edit-1.5b@local). If empty, inline completions are disabled.

llamaCopilot.inlineCompletionMaxInputByt.description: Maximum total input size in bytes (UTF-8) for inline completion requests (prefix + suffix + context).

llamaCopilot.inlineCompletionTimeoutMs.description: Request timeout in milliseconds for inline completion requests. No suggestion is shown on timeout.

llamaCopilot.inlineCompletionDebounceMs.description: Debounce delay in milliseconds before sending an automatic (as-you-type) inline completion request. Invoked completions are not debounced.

llamaCopilot.inlineCompletionIncludeCont.description: Use open tab contents to provide better inline completions.

llamaCopilot.inlineCompletionPrompt.default: Limit completion to a few words.
llamaCopilot.inlineCompletionPrompt.description: Text sent as the llama-server POST /infill "prompt" field (instruction after the FIM middle marker). Affects ghost inline completions only. Clear to omit. If an endpoint or model merges a "prompt" into requestBody, that value overrides this setting.

llamaCopilot.debug.modelListFetch.description: Enable debug logging for model list fetch operations

llamaCopilot.debug.completion.description: Enable debug logging for completion requests

llamaCopilot.debug.tokenization.description: Enable debug logging for tokenization operations

llamaCopilot.debug.rulesMatching.description: Enable debug logging for rules matching operations

llamaCopilot.debug.toolCalls.description: Enable debug logging for tool calls

llamaCopilot.debug.inlineCompletion.description: Enable debug logging for inline completion requests, cancellations, and errors

llamaCopilot.smtp.enabled.description: When enabled, email tool results for configured tool names (e.g. runSubagent) via SMTP.

llamaCopilot.smtp.port.description: SMTP port (e.g. 587 STARTTLS, 465 TLS, or a custom port).

llamaCopilot.smtp.secure.description: If true, connect with TLS immediately (typical for port 465). If false, use STARTTLS when offered (typical for 587).

llamaCopilot.smtp.from.description: From address (RFC 5322). Required when SMTP email is enabled.

llamaCopilot.smtp.tls.rejectUnauthorized.description: Reject TLS certificates that fail verification. Set false only for testing with self-signed CAs.

llamaCopilot.smtp.toolNames.description: Tool names whose results are emailed (e.g. runSubagent). Exact match.

llamaCopilot.smtp.maxBodyChars.description: Maximum characters for the tool result body in the email (truncation applied beyond this).

llamaCopilot.ssh.host.description: SSH host address for the remote Llama server.

llamaCopilot.ssh.privateKeyPath.description: Path to SSH private key file for authentication.

llamaCopilot.llamaServer.apiUrl.description: Llama server API URL (on the remote host).

llamaCopilot.llamaServer.startCommand.description: Command to start the llama server on the remote host.

llamaCopilot.llamaServer.stopCommand.description: Command to stop the llama server on the remote host.

llamaCopilot.llamaServer.shutdownCommand.description: Command to shutdown the remote host.

llamaCopilot.llamaServer.startModelRefre.description: Delay in milliseconds after starting the llama server before refreshing the model list. The start command returns immediately, so this waits for the server process to be ready.
